title: Lines, Containers, Headings, Paragraphs, Lists, Quotes, and Fenced Code · Runtime Lens: Block Precedence
summary: Blank lines erase structure, list markers nest by guesswork, headings skip source evidence, a fence closes with the wrong marker, and paragraphs swallow following blocks. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the block precedence increment in line-oriented block parser with explicit continuation rules. Learners progress through prediction, explicit t…
objectives: Apply named block-start and continuation precedence so paragraphs, headings, quotes, lists, thematic breaks, and code resolve consistently.
Represent line content, indentation, blankness, offset, tab expansion, newline, and container prefix without discarding source positions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-block-parsing-2
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-block-parsing-2.
# Case 7417: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def worked_static_site_block_parsing_2(lines: int = 28, consumed_lines: int = 28, blocks: int = 9, invalid_containers: int = 0, unclosed_fences: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "readerworked2",
        "source7417",
        "parse2dekrow",
        "routeworked27417",
        "artifact7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if lines < 1 or consumed_lines < 0:
        return False, consumed_lines, "line-count"
    if consumed_lines != lines:
        return False, consumed_lines, "line-consumption"
    if blocks < 1 or blocks > lines:
        return False, blocks, "block-shape"
    if invalid_containers > 0 or unclosed_fences > 0:
        return False, invalid_containers + unclosed_fences, "block-boundary"
    assert consumed_lines == lines
    return True, blocks, "bounded-block-tree"

# Evidence: ssg-block-precedence-1x2kqp
# Competency: ssg-block-precedence.
# Case 8650: a multilingual public-service handbook migration.
# Operating constraint: preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def evidence_ssg_block_precedence_1x2kqp(lines: int = 28, consumed_lines: int = 28, blocks: int = 9, invalid_containers: int = 0, unclosed_fences: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1x2kqp = "1x2kqp-pqk2x1-8650"
    assert evidence_variant_1x2kqp
    evidence_axes_1x2kqp = (
        "reader1x2kqp",
        "source8650",
        "parsepqk2x1",
        "route1x2kqp8650",
        "artifact86501x2kqp",
        "recoverypqk2x18650",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1x2kqp)) == 6
    if lines < 1 or consumed_lines < 0:
        return False, consumed_lines, "line-count"
    if consumed_lines != lines:
        return False, consumed_lines, "line-consumption"
    if blocks < 1 or blocks > lines:
        return False, blocks, "block-shape"
    if invalid_containers > 0 or unclosed_fences > 0:
        return False, invalid_containers + unclosed_fences, "block-boundary"
    assert consumed_lines == lines
    return True, blocks, "bounded-block-tree"

options.text: For repair the block precedence defect, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Block Precedence, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the block precedence defect evidence boundary.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Block Precedence model · Theory Block Precedence
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Block Precedence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 8386 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to parse headings, paragraphs, containers, and fenced code with explicit line consumption; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Apply named block-start and continuation precedence so paragraphs, headings, quotes, lists, thematic breaks, and code resolve consistently.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Block Precedence model · Theory Block Precedence.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Block Precedence model · Theory Block Precedence, keep Block Precedence connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Apply named block-start and continuation precedence so paragraphs, headings, quotes, lists, thematic breaks, and code resolve consistently.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Block Precedence model · Theory Block Precedence, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Block Precedence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Block Precedence model · Theory Block Precedence, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 8386 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to parse headings, paragraphs, containers, and fenced code with explicit line consumption; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Apply named block-start and continuation precedence so paragraphs, headings, quotes, lists, thematic breaks, and code resolve consistently.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Block Precedence model · Theory Block Precedence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Trying each block regex in any order produces the same document.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-block-precedence-1x2kqp(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_block_precedence_1x2kqp\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?lines < 1 or consumed_lines < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?consumed_lines != lines)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?blocks < 1 or blocks > lines)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?invalid_containers > 0 or unclosed_fences > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
